assessments.rationale: IPPs pre-qualify sites via Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung; MV capacity is a site-selection gate, not a market-level barrier.
221118 projects are greenfield/brownfield sized specifically for BESS footprint; 40–80 m²/MWh is a routine design parameter.
VdS fire concept + Feuerwehr plan is baseline deliverable for every BESS IPP in Germany — market-standard practice, not a barrier.
Containerized BESS with NOVEC 1230 + deflagration venting + compartmentalization is the de-facto architecture for this market.
6–12 months for FGW TR8 / VDE-AR-N 4110 is the routine critical path for 221118 projects; Mein Solar has native capability.

IPP buyers increasingly require supply-chain transparency under CRMA (2025) and EU Battery Regulation. Not a physical barrier — a compliance/contractual overhead. One-time organizational build with marginal per-project cost.
assessments.mitigation: Establish dual-source procurement (primary CN + secondary KR/EU) for LFP cells, Build EU Battery Regulation Digital Product Passport (DPP) compliance pipeline at FAT, Bundle CRMA-compatible sourcing documentation into EPC deliverables

assessments.rationale: German site climate (-15 to +38 °C historic) sits inside the -20 to +50 °C envelope; container HVAC handles extremes.
IPP arbitrage/FCR/aFRR revenue models explicitly price 85–90% AC-AC RTE. It is a design parameter, not a barrier to participation.
221118 IPP market self-selects for projects inside €250–400/kWh envelope; 2026 LFP turnkey pricing ≈ €200–320/kWh installed — well inside threshold.
